data.four_set_effect: Increases Charged Attack CRIT Rate by 30%.

circlet_of_logos.desc: A reed coronet that emanates the spring breeze.
circlet_of_logos.title: Crown of Parting

flower_of_life.desc: A small cerulean flower with somebody's ribbon pinned to the stem.
flower_of_life.title: Heart of Comradeship

goblet_of_eonothem.desc: A plain porcelain goblet that was once brimming with joyous brews.
goblet_of_eonothem.title: Goblet of the Sojourner

plume_of_death.desc: A blue arrow fletching imbued with the sentiment of travelers that had once faded into the horizon.
plume_of_death.title: Feather of Homecoming

sands_of_eon.desc: A sundial that has survived the ages, always silently recording the cycles of the sun and moon as they pass through the sky.
sands_of_eon.title: Sundial of the Sojourner

list.desc: The traveler from a distant land pinned this flower close to his chest.</br>The traveler left a life of great privilege to pursue a wandering, unburdened life.</br>In a foreign land, by a clear lake of sweet waters, he met a frowning maiden.</br>"A traveler from afar? ...Huh. Not like it matters."</br>"You're a musician? Well then, pray do not insult me with empty praises or song."</br>"Just remember me as I am."</br>"As one about to be offered up as a sacrifice for the 'festival.'"</br>The traveler who had abandoned his homeland pinned this flower close to his chest.</br>He wished to remind himself to love no one, and become attached to nothing.</br>Yet he would remember that maiden as he had promised, and walk into the fire for her sake...</br>
list.title: Heart of Comradeship

list.desc: The departing traveler would take this crown of willow branches as a final memento.</br>With it, he would remember his lover, now departed like a dandelion on the breeze.</br>The traveler was the wandering lyrist, and that maiden in the country of wine was a prisoner of the nobility.</br>For reasons he knew not himself, his lyre-song revealed his true heart.</br>"What would move me, you ask? ...Well, it would be your smile."</br>"I've never known you to smile, after all. Not even once."</br>"I will break the chains that bind you."</br>"When that time comes, would you let me see you smile?"</br>"Yes, and thank you. It would be wonderful indeed if you could do that."</br>Pretty words, but who could trust them, coming from a singer?</br>
list.title: Crown of Parting

list.desc: A lyre has 4 strings.</br>The days he spent with his comrades in the "troupe" were probably the happiest in his life of wandering.</br>At first, it was just a chance meeting with the conductor — but for the invisible strings of fate.</br>First came the girl who used a flute as a sword, and then that fellow Kreuzlied — one by one, the traveler met them all.</br>In the joyous tavern, the lyrist introduced a maiden he had met by chance to his comrades amidst the singing and dancing.</br>The drunken traveler strummed the strings and sang in a loud voice.</br>With them by his side, he would never have to travel alone again...</br>Perhaps it would be alright to walk with them till journey's end.</br>
list.title: Goblet of the Sojourner

list.desc: Hope was ripped to shreds by ruthless might, and promises of reunion disappeared like fleeting shadows.</br>The wandering traveler had once against lost a place to call home.</br>The condescending evil stole his love's joy,</br>And the endless conflict ground down his irreverent soul.</br>Those gentle, playful rhythms of revelry,</br>Now turned to sharp, icy clangs.</br>For his comrades, for his best friend, for the tables around which none would again share wine.</br>For freedom, for life, and for revenge against the evil that had taken her smile.</br>Thus resolved, the traveler plucked his last string, and fired his final arrow.</br>As he grew accustomed to life in this foreign land, he gazed up at the azure sky.</br>Ah, so the sky here was the same as the one back in his homeland...</br>
list.title: Feather of Homecoming

list.desc: The traveler pursued both fate and the inexorable passage of time.</br>The troupe that resisted the aristocracy — and their end — could be seen the eternal flow of time.</br>In the course of a long journey, even the most well-made watch might cease to work.</br>The only unfailing marker of time was the passage of the sun and moon.</br>So to chase formless time, the traveler could only pursue light itself.</br>The great halls of the nobility, and the rebellious troupe with no room to lay their heads.</br>Both were churned just the same by the flow of time, and both like fleeting dreams raced towards the same destruction.</br>Under the moonless night sky, the dark shadows drew upon the outlander's tired face.</br>"The songs are spent like arrows few, to the choir the curtain calls."</br>"Shall I ever see you smile anew, when the tower in the city square falls?"</br>
list.title: Sundial of the Sojourner
